title: India's peak power demand hits 240 GW in April amid intense heatwave - Deccan Herald
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Assam
full_text:
New Delhi: With rising temperatures many parts of the country, India's peak power demand surged to a seasonal high of 240 GW on Thursday.

This is highest level so far in the summer season, says officials of Power Ministry.

According to data from the Ministry of Power, the country's peak power demand met (the highest supply in a day) was recorded a tad higher at 240.12 GW on April 23, up from 239.70 GW on April 22, the highest during this summer.

.Delhi court rejects jailed MP Engineer Rashid's interim bail plea to meet ailing father.

Though the record high of 250 GW of peak power demand was recorded in May 2024, a similar trend may witness this year too as several states already witnessing heat wave, says officials.

Increased consumption also driven by usage of air conditioners and agricultural consumption.

The surge indicated that an early onset of heat stress in key regions, particularly in northern and western India, where temperatures have already begun breaching seasonal averages.

Last year, the peak power demand was 242.77 GW, which was recorded in June, 2025. In the first fortnight of this month, the peak power demand remained lower than the 235.32 GW recorded in April last year.

The power Ministry officials said that the demand and consumption of electricity will increase further in coming days as the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) said, there will be heatwave conditions over major parts of northwest, central and eastern India till this weekend.

During the last summer (April 2025 onwards), the peak power demand reached 242.77 GW in June but stayed below the government's estimate of 277 GW. The power ministry has estimated the peak power demand of about 270 GW during the summer season this year.

title: School timings changed in five more districts due to heatwave - The Times of India

full_text:
- School timings changed in six more districts due to heatwave
Trending
Jaipur: Amid heatwave predictions, school timings were revised in five more districts Friday.In Bundi, effective April 24, operating hours for all govt and private schools for classes 1 to 8 will be from 7.30am to 12pm. The schedule will remain in force till the commencement of summer vacations.In Alwar, timings for all govt and private schools from pre-primary to class 8 have been changed to 7.30am to 12pm from April 27. However, timings for classes 9 to 12 and staff will remain unchanged. In Jaisalmer and Jodhpur, the same schedule came into effect from Friday for students up to class 8.In Karauli, schools for classes 1 to 8 will function from 7.30am to 11am from April 27 to May 16. In Phalodi, schools from nursery to class 8 will operate from 7.30am to 12 noon from Saturday.Meanwhile, the Jaipur district administration revised working hours under the Mahatma Gandhi NREGA scheme in view of severe heatwave conditions. From May 2 until further orders, work timings will be 7am to 3pm, with a rest break from 11am to 12pm.School timings have already been changed in Jaipur for students up to class 8, and in Kota for all students up to class 12.
